0:99215.title: Common Billing Errors to Check
0:99215.citation: Billing for a more expensive procedure than what was performed. Example: Billing CPT 99215 (complex office visit, ~$250) when CPT 99213 (low-complexity visit, ~$110) was provided. Red flag: Charges seem disproportionate to the service received.
0:99215.why_it_applies: The anomaly flags CPT 99215 as an overcharge at 2.5x the Medicare rate, which is a textbook upcoding red flag — the KB explicitly uses CPT 99215 as its primary upcoding example, making this a directly applicable protection.

0:99215.title: How to Dispute a Medical Bill — Step-by-Step
0:99215.citation: Use CareGap's CMS PFS pricing tool to compare each CPT code's charge against the Medicare-allowed amount. A charge significantly above the Medicare rate (e.g., 3-5x or more) may be excessive.
0:99215.why_it_applies: The billed amount of $316.00 is 2.5x the Medicare-allowed rate of $125.13, which falls within the range the KB identifies as potentially excessive, giving the patient grounds to formally dispute this charge through the outlined step-by-step process.

letter.subject: Formal Dispute of Overcharge — Account #847291 — Jane M. Rodriguez — Date of Service 03/15/2024
letter.body:
Jane M. Rodriguez
[Your Address]

Date: [Today's Date]

Riverside Medical Associates
1200 Health Pkwy, Suite 300
Princeton, NJ 08544

Re: Formal Billing Dispute
Patient Name: Jane M. Rodriguez
Date of Service: 03/15/2024
Account Number: 847291
Insurance: BlueCross PPO
Total Amount Billed: $748.00

To Whom It May Concern,

I am writing on behalf of patient Jane M. Rodriguez to formally dispute a charge appearing on the bill associated with Account Number 847291 for services rendered on March 15, 2024. After a careful review of the itemized charges against established Medicare reimbursement benchmarks, I have identified a billing anomaly that requires your immediate attention and written response.

DISPUTED CHARGE

CPT Code 99215 — Office Visit, Level 5 (High Complexity)
Amount Billed: $316.00
Medicare Physician Fee Schedule Allowed Amount: $125.13
Multiplier: Approximately 2.5x the Medicare-allowed rate

CPT code 99215 represents a high-complexity office visit and is among the highest-level evaluation and management codes available for outpatient encounters. A charge of $316.00 against a Medicare-allowed rate of $125.13 represents a significant deviation from established reimbursement norms. This discrepancy raises a concern that the service billed may not accurately reflect the complexity of care actually provided, or that an error in code selection has occurred. A common and well-documented billing error involves the use of a higher-complexity CPT code — such as 99215 — when the service rendered more accurately corresponds to a lower-complexity code, such as CPT 99213, which reflects a low-complexity visit. We request documentation confirming that the clinical record supports the medical necessity and documentation requirements for a Level 5 visit under current AMA CPT guidelines and CMS Evaluation and Management documentation standards effective January 1, 2021.

REQUEST FOR ITEMIZED RECORDS

Pursuant to HIPAA, 45 C.F.R. Section 164.524, Jane M. Rodriguez has the right to access her medical and billing records. We hereby formally request a complete itemized bill listing every CPT code billed, the corresponding charge, any adjustments applied, and the supporting clinical documentation for the visit dated March 15, 2024. This request includes, but is not limited to, the provider's notes, the time and complexity documentation used to support the selection of CPT 99215, and any explanation of benefits received from BlueCross PPO.

APPLICABLE LEGAL CONTEXT

While the No Surprises Act, effective January 1, 2022, primarily governs surprise billing in the context of out-of-network services, its broader consumer protection framework underscores the obligation of providers to bill accurately and transparently. Additionally, New Jersey's Consumer Fraud Act (N.J.S.A. 56:8-1 et seq.) prohibits deceptive practices in commerce, which may encompass systematic upcoding or misrepresentation of services rendered. We raise these frameworks not as immediate legal claims, but to underscore the seriousness with which this dispute is being pursued and the patient's awareness of her rights.

REQUESTED RESOLUTION

We respectfully request that Riverside Medical Associates take the following actions within 30 days of receipt of this letter:

1. Provide a complete itemized bill for Account Number 847291 as described above.
2. Provide the clinical documentation supporting the selection of CPT 99215 for the March 15, 2024 encounter.
3. Conduct an internal audit of the disputed charge and issue a written explanation of the billing determination.
4. If the charge is found to be in error, issue a corrected bill and, if applicable, a refund of any overpayment already collected from the patient or her insurer.

Please direct your written response to the address listed above within 30 calendar days of the date of this letter. We ask that all correspondence be provided in writing.

NEXT STEPS IF UNRESOLVED

If we do not receive a substantive written response within 30 days, or if the dispute is not resolved to the patient's satisfaction, Jane M. Rodriguez reserves the right to escalate this matter through the following channels without further notice:

1. Filing a complaint with the New Jersey Division of Consumer Affairs and the Office of the New Jersey Attorney General.
2. Filing a complaint with the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) regarding unfair or deceptive billing practices.
3. Filing a complaint with the New Jersey Department of Banking and Insurance, which oversees insurance-related billing disputes involving BlueCross PPO.
4. Reporting the billing anomaly to the CMS Medicare Fraud, Waste, and Abuse hotline if the evidence supports a pattern of upcoding.

This letter is being sent as a formal record of dispute. We trust that Riverside Medical Associates will address this matter promptly and in good faith.

Respectfully,

[Patient Advocate Name]
On Behalf of Jane M. Rodriguez
Account Number: 847291
[Your Address]
[Phone Number]
[Email Address]
letter.recipient_block:
To:
Riverside Medical Associates
1200 Health Pkwy, Suite 300
Princeton, NJ 08544

roadmap.title: Request Itemized Bill with CPT Codes
roadmap.detail: Contact Riverside Medical Associates billing department and request a fully itemized bill listing every charge with its corresponding CPT code, date of service, and provider name. You are legally entitled to this document and it is the foundation for any dispute.

roadmap.title: Review Flagged Charge Against Medicare Rates
roadmap.detail: CareGap has already identified 1 flagged charge contributing to an estimated $190.87 in potential overcharges on your $748.00 bill. Cross-reference the specific line item on your itemized bill against the Medicare reimbursement rate for that CPT code to confirm the discrepancy before proceeding.

roadmap.title: Send a Formal Dispute Letter to Billing Department
roadmap.detail: Write a concise dispute letter to Riverside Medical Associates citing the specific CPT code, the amount charged, the benchmark rate, and the dollar difference. Request a corrected bill or written justification for the variance, and send it via certified mail to create a paper trail.

roadmap.title: File a Grievance with Your Insurance Provider
roadmap.detail: If you are insured, submit a formal billing grievance to your health plan with copies of the itemized bill, your dispute letter, and any response from Riverside Medical Associates. Your insurer has a financial interest in resolving overbilling and can apply additional pressure on the provider.

roadmap.title: File a Complaint with Your State Insurance Commissioner or Attorney General
roadmap.detail: If Riverside Medical Associates does not respond or refuses to correct the bill within 30 days, file a formal complaint with your state's insurance commissioner or attorney general's consumer protection office. Reference the specific billing error, the applicable consumer protection guidelines, and attach all prior correspondence.

summary:
# Your Medical Bill Summary

You were charged $316 for a complex office visit, but Medicare says that same visit should only cost $125.13—that's a difference of about $191 that looks like an overcharge. Out of your $748 total bill, we flagged this one charge as the main problem that's worth looking into. I'd recommend calling Riverside Medical Associates to ask why they charged so much more than the standard rate for this visit.
